_ = Finset.affineCombination k (Finset.univ : Finset { x // x ∈ s }) (p ∘ Subtype.val) (fun i => w i : { x // x ∈ s } → k) := by
              simp [Finset.affineCombination_eq_sum_simplex]
              <;>
              congr <;> ext i <;> simp_all [Subtype.coe_mk]
            _ = (Finset.affineCombination k Finset.univ (p ∘ Subtype.val)) w := by
              simp [Finset.affineCombination_eq_sum_simplex]

    · -- Prove the reverse direction: ∃ w, ∑ i ∈ s, w i = 1 ∧ p₀ = s.affineCombination k p w → p₀ ∈ affineSpan k (p '' s)
      intro h
      -- Assume there exists a weight function w such that ∑ i ∈ s, w i = 1 and p₀ = s.affineCombination k p w
      rcases h with ⟨w, hw, hpw⟩
      -- Show that p₀ is in the affine span of p '' s
      have h₂ : p₀ ∈ affineSpan k (p '' s) := by
        -- Use the fact that the affine combination of points in p '' s is in the affine span
        have h₃ : s.affineCombination k p w ∈ affineSpan k (p '' s) := by
          -- Prove that the affine combination is in the affine span of p '' s
          classical
          have h₄ : ∀ i ∈ s, p i ∈ (p '' s) := by
            intro i hi
            -- For each i in s, p i is in the image of p over s
            exact ⟨i, hi, rfl⟩
          -- Use the fact that the affine combination of points in p '' s is in the affine span of p '' s
          have h₅ : s.affineCombination k p w ∈ affineSpan k (p '' s) := by
            apply affineCombination_mem_affineSpan
            intro i hi
            exact h₄ i hi
          exact h₅
        -- Since p₀ equals the affine combination, it is in the affine span of p '' s
        calc
          p₀ = s.affineCombination k p w := by rw [hpw]
          _ ∈ affineSpan k (p '' s) := h₃
      exact h₂
  }
